summary.intent: Investigate and fix GitHub issue #556 where images sent via Telegram to Mistral/Qwen models were being silently stripped instead of forwarded to the model.
summary.outcome: Fixed `supports_vision_for_model()` in `crates/providers/src/lib.rs` by flipping from an allowlist to a denylist approach (default-true), committed, pushed, created PR #558, and addressed a follow-up review comment about `gpt-4-vision-preview` being incorrectly denied.

repo: Vision support is gated by `supports_vision_for_model()` in `crates/providers/src/lib.rs` — if it returns false, images are silently stripped via `sanitize_tool_result()` in `runner.rs` before being sent to the model.
The project uses a 'beads' issue tracker (backed by Dolt) alongside GitHub issues — both need to be updated when fixing bugs.
Worktrees are stored under `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/` with branch-named subdirectories.

code.finding: `supports_vision_for_model()` was an allowlist-based function; changed to denylist with default-true. Known text-only denylisted prefixes: `codestral`, `gpt-3.5`, `text-`, `gpt-4-` (excluding `gpt-4-turbo` and `gpt-4-vision-preview`), `glm-` without 'v' suffix.
`tool_result_to_content()` calls `sanitize_tool_result()` when `supports_vision=false`, which silently drops image data — the silent failure mode that motivated the denylist flip.

workflow: Default-deny for capability flags causes silent failures (images stripped with no error); default-allow causes visible API errors — prefer default-allow when the visible failure mode is more diagnosable.
Greptile bot posts automated review comments on PRs that can surface real bugs (caught the `gpt-4-vision-preview` regression).
Dolt-backed beads issue tracker can be intermittently unavailable; safe to defer closing issues and retry later.

friction: Dolt/beads server was down for an extended period, requiring multiple retried `gh beads close` calls and a manual wait before the issue could be closed.
The `gpt-4-` prefix denylist accidentally blocked `gpt-4-vision-preview` — caught only by a post-merge PR review comment, requiring a follow-up commit.
open_items: The `MISTRAL_MODELS` list in `crates/providers/src/lib.rs` may not include all Mistral vision models — was noted as a potential follow-up but not addressed.
Denylist of text-only models is manually maintained and will need updates as new text-only model families are released.
